StrengthMultiplier: Multiplies the HP of the Lord. Standard = 1, Rat = 0.5, Wolf = 1.5
DotColour: The color of the dots indicating the lords strength. Vanilla uses yellow for weak AIs and blue for strong ones
DotCount: The amount of dots indicating the lords strength. Vanilla uses one dot per 0.1 of multiplier
Type: The type of lord that spawns on the keep. Either Arab (Saladin) or Europ (Richard)
ArabArcher: Amount of ArabArchers that spawn by the keep at game start
Slave: Amount of Slaves that spawn by the keep at game start
Slinger: Amount of Slingers that spawn by the keep at game start
Assassin: Amount of Assassins that spawn by the keep at game start
HorseArcher: Amount of HorseArchers that spawn by the keep at game start
ArabSwordsman: Amount of ArabSwordsmans that spawn by the keep at game start
FireThrower: Amount of FireThrowers that spawn by the keep at game start
EuropArcher: Amount of EuropArchers that spawn by the keep at game start
Spearman: Amount of Spearmans that spawn by the keep at game start
Maceman: Amount of Macemans that spawn by the keep at game start
Crossbowman: Amount of Crossbowmans that spawn by the keep at game start
Pikeman: Amount of Pikemans that spawn by the keep at game start
Swordsman: Amount of Swordsmans that spawn by the keep at game start
Knight: Amount of Knights that spawn by the keep at game start
Monk: Amount of Monks that spawn by the keep at game start
WallDecoration: The type of decoration the lord uses when placing flg in the aiv-editor. 8 = oil pouring out with fire, 9 = burning flames, 10 = small flag, 11 = big flag, 12 = medium flag, 13 = crusader flag, 14 = braziers, 15 = decorative skulls, 21 = steam clouds, 22 = disease clouds, 26 = fire explosions, 28 = ravens flying down, 29 = medium rocks landing in water, 30 = small rocks hitting structures, 31 = small rocks landing in water, 40 = zero gold rising note, 41 = zero popularity rising note, 42 = blank rising note, 43 = blank rising note
CriticalPopularity: Below this popularity, the AI sells goods aggressively to raise gold. 10000 = 100 popularity.
LowestPopularity: Below this popularity, AI sets taxes towards TaxesMin and gives extra rations until HighestPopularity is reached. 10000 = 100 popularity.
HighestPopularity: Above this popularity, AI increases taxes until a stable popularity modifier of up to -3 is achieved. 10000 = 100 popularity.
Farm1: type of the 1. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm2: type of the 2. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm3: type of the 3. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm4: type of the 4. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm5: type of the 5. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm6: type of the 6. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm7: type of the 7. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm8: type of the 8. Farm that the AI builds. After the 8. farm the AI will start over from the top. Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
PopulationPerFarm: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one farm. The first farm is always build at 0 population. Hop farms dont count towards this value and are built whenever next in queue
PopulationPerWoodcutter: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one woodcutter. When the AI reaches this amount for the first time two are build instead.
PopulationPerQuarry: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one Quarry. When the AI reaches this amount for the first time two are build instead.
PopulationPerIronmine: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one Ironmine. When the AI reaches this amount for the first time two are build instead.
PopulationPerPitchrig: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one Pitchrig.
MaxQuarries: Maximum amount of Quarrys the AI will build
MaxIronmines: Maximum amount of Ironmines the AI will build
MaxWoodcutters: Maximum amount of Woodcutters the AI will build
MaxPitchrigs: Maximum amount of Pitchrigs the AI will build
MaxFarms: Maximum amount of Farms the AI will build. Hop Farms dont count towards this value.
BuildInterval: The speed at which the AI builds. Counts for everything, AIV buildings, non-AIV buildings and all kinds of repairs. 1 is the fastest.
ResourceRebuildDelay: The time the AI waits before trying to rebuild a non-AIV recource building. When rebuilding a Farm the AI builds the next one in the queue until it has build a different Farm then a Hop Farm.
MaxFood: The maximum amount stored of Apples, Cheese, Meat, Bread, Wheat and Hop (+ResourceVariance on top). Amount choosen is per type, NOT combined.
MinimumApples: If the amount of apples stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] apples, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy apples. If = -1 the AI will sell all apples.
MinimumCheese: If the amount of cheese stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] cheese, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy cheese. If = -1 the AI will sell all cheese.
MinimumBread: If the amount of bread stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] bread, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy bread. If = -1 the AI will sell all bread. Can be better to leave at 0 and buy wheat instead with bread-eco
MinimumWheat: If the amount of wheat stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] wheat, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy wheat. If = -1 the AI will sell all wheat
MinimumHop: If the amount of hop stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] hop, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy hop. If = -1 the AI will sell all hop.
TradeAmountFood: The amount of food the AI buys at a time. At a low value each purchase is cheaper (more reliable to happen) but may increases the amount of purchases needed (slower restocking of goods, may cause short shortages). The AI only buys 1 type of food at a Time.
TradeAmountEquipment: The amount of Weapons the AI buys at a time. At a low value each purchase is cheaper (more reliable to happen) but may increases the amount of purchases needed (slower restocking of weapons, may cause short shortages). The AI only buys 1 type of Equipment at a Time.
AIRequestDelay: The frequency with which the AI asks the player for resources (lower value -> more frequent).
MinimumGoodsRequiredAfterTrade: On recource request of a player: the amount of a recource, that needs to be left over after the trade for the AI to agree to it. Otherwise the AI will decline the trade.
DoubleRationsFoodThreshold: If the combined amount of Apples, Cheese, Meat and Bread is bigger than this value, the AI will give double rations.
MaxWood: The maximum amount stored of Wood (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxStone: The maximum amount stored of Stone (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxResourceOther: The maximum amount stored of Iron, Pitch and Flour (+ResourceVariance on top). Amount choosen is per type, NOT combined.
MaxEquipment: The maximum amount stored of Weapons and Armors (+ResourceVariance on top). Amount choosen is per type, NOT combined.
MaxBeer: The maximum amount stored of Beer (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxResourceVariance: The deviation allowed from any 'Max[Recource]' Value.
RecruitGoldThreshold: The minimum amount of gold the AI needs to have, before it starts recruiting units.
BlacksmithSetting: Decides what the AIs Blacksmith settings are set to. If 'Both' is chosen, the first will be set to Swords, the second to Maces, the third to Swords, etc.
FletcherSetting: Decides what the AIs Fletcher settings are set to. If 'Both' is chosen, the first will be set to Bows, the second to Crossbows, the third to Bows, etc.
PoleturnerSetting: Decides what the AIs Poleturner settings are set to. If 'Both' is chosen, the first will be set to Spears, the second to Pikes, the third to Spears, etc.
SellResource01: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource02: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource03: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource04: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource05: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource06: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource07: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource08: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource09: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource10: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource11: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource12: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource13: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource14: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
SellResource15: Recources that, if obtained, are always insantly sold by the AI.
DefWallPatrolRallyTime: The time that Def units other than DefWalls wait until walking to their next unit spot in the AIV. Not all DefWall units can patrol.
DefWallPatrolGroups: How many Groups of Def Wall units patrol trough their AIV spots.
DefSiegeEngineGoldThreshold: Amount of Gold the AI needs to have before it build AIV Siege Engines
DefSiegeEngineBuildDelay: The Amount of time the AI wait before rebuilding destroyed AIV Siege Engines. Low value -> less time.
RecruitProbDefDefault: The probability the AI to recruit Def units in the Default State.
RecruitProbDefWeak: The probability the AI to recruit Def units in the Weak State.
RecruitProbDefStrong: The probability the AI to recruit Def units in the Strong State.
RecruitProbRaidDefault: The probability the AI to recruit Raid units in the Default State.
RecruitProbRaidWeak: The probability the AI to recruit Raid units in the Weak State.
RecruitProbRaidStrong: The probability the AI to recruit Raid units in the Strong State.
RecruitProbAttackDefault: The probability the AI to recruit Attack units in the Default State.
RecruitProbAttackWeak: The probability the AI to recruit Attack units in the Weak State.
RecruitProbAttackStrong: The probability the AI to recruit Attack units in the Strong State. Sortie units always have priority to be recruited before anything else.
SortieUnitRangedMin: The minimum amount of Ranged units the AI sends. Ranged Sorties scale up the more often they are killed. Usually around 25 units more than the given value are recruit later during the game.
SortieUnitRanged: The Type of Ranged unit the AI uses to attack units that kill its peasants.
SortieUnitMeleeMin: The exact amount of Melee units the AI always send. Sortie units always have priority to be recruited before anything else.
SortieUnitMelee: The Type of Melee unit the AI uses to attack units that kill its peasants.
DefDiggingUnitMax: Maximum amount of Digging units the AI recruits. Digging units have priority to be recruited before Def, Raid and Attack units as long as the AI or an ally has moat planned.
DefDiggingUnit: Type of unit the AI uses to dig its defensive moat or the moat of an ally.
RecruitInterval: The amount of time the AI waits after recruiting an unit before recruiting the next unit while in the default state. 1 is the fastest anything above is slower.
RecruitIntervalWeak: The amount of time the AI waits after recruiting an unit before recruiting the next unit while in the weak state.  1 is the fastest anything above is slower.
RecruitIntervalStrong: The amount of time the AI waits after recruiting an unit before recruiting the next unit while in the strong state.  1 is the fastest anything above is slower.
DefTotal: The total amount of Def units the AI recruits.
OuterPatrolGroupsCount: The amount of Def unit Groups other than DefWalls that patrol around the economy.
OuterPatrolGroupsMove: Either true of false whether the Def units other than DefWalls should patrol around the economy or stand still (often by the Quarries).
OuterPatrolRallyDelay: The time that Def units other than DefWalls wait until walking to the next location.
DefWalls: How many units of the DefTotal go to their AIV spots in the castle. The rest will Patrol the economy
DefUnit1: type of the 1. Def unit that the AI recruits. If it cant afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recuit the next one in order.
DefUnit2: type of the 2. Def unit that the AI recruits.
DefUnit3: type of the 3. Def unit that the AI recruits.
DefUnit4: type of the 4. Def unit that the AI recruits.
DefUnit5: type of the 5. Def unit that the AI recruits.
DefUnit6: type of the 6. Def unit that the AI recruits.
DefUnit7: type of the 7. Def unit that the AI recruits.
DefUnit8: type of the 8. Def unit that the AI recruits.
RaidUnitsBase: The amount of units the AI uses to raid an opponents economy buildings and Granary/Amoury.
RaidUnitsRandom: The amount of units the AI may add on top of the 'RaidUnitsBase'if it feels strong and has alot of gold
RaidUnit1: type of the 1. Raid unit that the AI recruits. If it cant afford to recruit a unit slot it tries to recuit the next one in order.
RaidUnit2: type of the 2. Raid unit that the AI recruits.
RaidUnit3: type of the 3. Raid unit that the AI recruits.
RaidUnit4: type of the 4. Raid unit that the AI recruits.
RaidUnit5: type of the 5. Raid unit that the AI recruits.
RaidUnit6: type of the 6. Raid unit that the AI recruits.
RaidUnit7: type of the 7. Raid unit that the AI recruits.
RaidUnit8: type of the 8. Raid unit that the AI recruits.
HarassingSiegeEngine1: type of the 1.Harrasing Siege Engine that the AI recruits. Harrasing Siege Engines are build when the AI can afford them and travel from the Engineers Guild to the Enemy Castle shooting Buildings on the Way. (FireBallistas also shoot units)
HarassingSiegeEngine2: type of the 2. Harrasing Siege Engine that the AI recruits.
HarassingSiegeEngine3: type of the 3. Harrasing Siege Engine that the AI recruits.
HarassingSiegeEngine4: type of the 4. Harrasing Siege Enginethat the AI recruits.
HarassingSiegeEngine5: type of the 5. Harrasing Siege Engine that the AI recruits.
HarassingSiegeEngine6: type of the 6. Harrasing Siege Engine that the AI recruits.
HarassingSiegeEngine7: type of the 7. Harrasing Siege Engine that the AI recruits.
HarassingSiegeEngine8: type of the 8. Harrasing Siege Engine that the AI recruits.
HarassingSiegeEnginesMax: Maximum amount of Harrasing Siege Engines that are on the field at the same time.
RaidRetargetDelay: Amount of time the AI waits with its raid Troops before targeting a new building
AttForceBase: Amount of Attack units the AI recruits before Attacking the Opponent. Increases with each Attack based on what is selected in the UCP, in Vanilla by 5 per Attack
AttForceRandom: The amount of units the AI may add on top of the 'AttForceBase'if it feels strong and has alot of gold.
AttForceSupportAllyThreshold: Amount of attack units that needs to be recruited before the AI executes Attack/Help commands by the Player
AttForceRallyPercentage: Amount (in%) of Units that gather at the Rally point before the AI starts its attack
Unknown129: near 0 impact. Amount(in%) of units that need to reach the rallypoint before the AI initiates a retreat, in case of their attack being deemed too weak to deal damage. Also the amount (in%) of those reatreating units that need to reach the keep before the recruitement for a new attack beginns.
AttAssaultDelay: Time the AI waits with its Attacking Units and Siege Engines at the Rally point after starting its attack before sending them to Attack.
AttUnitPatrolRecommandDelay: Time the AI waits before retreating after a Sucessfull attack. Should Normally be at 0 because the AI has no Reason to wait at the opponents Grave.
Unknown132: medium impact in team-games. Chance (in%) that the AI waits with an attack for an ally to attack as well. At later stages it can also make the AI attack before the full attack is recruited if an ally attacks an enemy, to join them.
SiegeEngine1: type of the 1. Siege Engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine2: type of the 2. Siege Engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine3: type of the 3. Siege Engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine4: type of the 4. Siege Engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine5: type of the 5. Siege Engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine6: type of the 6. Siege Engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine7: type of the 7. Siege Engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
SiegeEngine8: type of the 8. Siege Engine that the AI recruits for attacks.
CowThrowInterval: Amount of Stones a Catapult needs to throw before throwing a Cow instead
AttMaxEngineers: Maximum Engineers the AI uses to man the Siege Engines in the SiegeEngine Slots. The amount should be atleast as many as your Siege Engines combined need to be operated.
AttDiggingUnit: Unit that is used to dig up the enemys moat during an attack.
AttDiggingUnitMax: Maximum amount of Digging units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitVanguard: Attacking unit that runs to Attack the Enemy Castle before every other unit.
AttUnitVanguardMax: Maximum amount of Vanguard units that get recruited per attack.
AttMaxAssassins: Maximum amount of Assassins who try to climb enemy Gatehouses during a Siege.
AttMaxLaddermen: Maximum amount of Laddermen who try to put their Ladder at a Wall during a Siege.
AttMaxTunnelers: Maximum amount of Tunnelers who try to dig a tunnel in direction of the enemy keep.
AttUnitPatrol: Units who patrol around the enemy castle and shoot enemy Units.
AttUnitPatrolMax: Maximum amount of Patrol units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitPatrolGroupsCount: Amount of Groups, Patrol units split up into at the start of an attack
AttUnitBackup: Units who stay at the Rally point and only attack if there is a path to the enemy Keep. (If all other attackers die they retreat)
AttUnitBackupMax: Maximum amount of backup units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitBackupGroupsCount: Amount of groups, Backup units split up into when attacking the enemy Keep.
AttUnitEngage: Units who try to Attack Enemy units and Peasant instead of Walls.
AttUnitEngageMax: Maximum amount of Engage units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitSiegeDef: Units who stay with the Siege Engines during an attack.
AttUnitSiegeDefMax: Maximum amount of SiegeDef units that get recruited per attack.
AttUnitSiegeDefGroupsCount: Amount of Groups, SiegeDef units split up into during an attack.
AttUnitMain1: type of the 1. unit that the AI recruits for main group of attacks. Only these units will scale infinitly.
AttUnitMain2: type of the 2. unit that the AI recruits for main group of attacks. Only these units will scale infinitly
AttUnitMain3: type of the 3. unit that the AI recruits for main group of attacks. Only these units will scale infinitly
AttUnitMain4: type of the 4. unit that the AI recruits for main group of attacks. Only these units will scale infinitly
AttMaxDefault: The number of main army units that the AI CAN recuit before having to fully recruit ALL other specialist roles (AttLaddermen, AttAssassins, AttUnitPatrol etc.). If set to 0 all specialist roles will have to be recruited before any of the AttUnitMain can be recruited.
AttMainGroupsCount: Amount of Groups, AttUnitMain units split up into during an attack.
TargetChoice: Sets the way the AI chooses the target of an attack. Possible values are: Gold (Always attacks the enemy player with the largest amount of gold.), Balanced (Attacks the enemy based on a calculated 'weakness' score. This is the most complex and nuanced targeting type.), Closest (Always attacks the closest enemy, measured from keep to keep.), Any (Attacks the next enemy in the internal player list. This can appear random but follows a fixed order.), Player (Always targets a human player first. If no human players are enemies, it defaults to Closest.)
AIOxTethers_DisableInitialOxTether: 'False' means vanilla behavior, 'True' means do not built an ox tether everytime a quarry is (re)built.
AIOxTethers_Logic: Set the logic to apply. False means vanilla, True means dynamic
AIOxTethers_MaxOxTethers: The total amount of ox tethers for this AI will never go above this value.
AIOxTethers_DynamicMaxOxTethers: The total amount of ox tethers for this AI will never go above this value multiplied by the amount of quarries.
AIOxTethers_MinimumOxTethersPerQuarry: If fewer linked ox tethers take stones from this quarry than this value, an ox tether will be built for this quarry.
AIOxTethers_MaximumOxTethersPerQuarry: If the amount of linked ox tethers for a quarry is higher than this value, no ox tether is built for this quarry.
AIOxTethers_ThresholdStoneLoad: If `stones/linked ox tethers` goes above this value for a quarry, another ox tether is built for that quarry.
RunningUnits_Macemen: If true, maceman will always run, even when grouped with slower units. If false they will match the speed of the slowest unit in their group (like Vanilla)
RunningUnits_Spearmen: If true, spearman will always run, even when grouped with slower units. If false they will match the speed of the slowest unit in their group (like Vanilla).
RunningUnits_Slingers: If true, slingers will always run, even when grouped with slower units. If false they will match the speed of the slowest unit in their group (like Vanilla)
RunningUnits_Slaves: If true, slaves will always run, even when grouped with slower units. If false they will match the speed of the slowest unit in their group (like Vanilla)
